meta_title: For Freelancers: design with AI to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Design with AI to Scale SaaS Development
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'stuck with basic apps' plateau. Learn to design with AI using Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver enterprise-grade SaaS faster.
content.answer_block: To break past basic app limitations, integrate AI-driven design workflows using Antigravity for rapid UI prototyping and Claude Code for iterative architectural refinement. By leveraging LLMs to handle boilerplate and complex state management, you shift from manual coding to high-level system orchestration, allowing you to ship professional-grade SaaS products consistently.
content.intro:
The screen glows with the same boilerplate you’ve written a hundred times. You’re trapped in the 'tutorial loop,' building basic CRUD apps that feel like they belong in a 2015 portfolio. Your clients demand innovation, but you’re suffocating under the weight of manual implementation and recursive bugs.

It’s time to abandon the legacy workflow. By treating AI as your senior architectural partner, you can transcend the mundane. We aren't just writing code; we are orchestrating systems. This guide bridges the gap between intermediate freelancer and elite AI builder, turning every project into a masterclass of speed and precision.

steps.title: Architectural Blueprinting
steps.description: Define the system boundaries and data schemas before writing a single line of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the requirements for a multi-tenant SaaS dashboard. Generate a clean, normalized database schema in Prisma and outline the core API endpoints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a high-level technical specification document for a multi-tenant SaaS architecture based on the provided schema.'

steps.title: Component Design System
steps.description: Generate a cohesive UI library that ensures visual consistency across the entire application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Tailwind-based component library for a SaaS dashboard, including sidebar navigation, data tables, and modal overlays with a focus on accessibility.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Create a set of reusable UI components in src/components/ui using Shadcn/UI patterns.'

steps.title: State Management Implementation
steps.description: Offload complex state synchronization to AI to prevent race conditions and bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a robust state management solution for a real-time analytics feed using TanStack Query, ensuring data consistency and optimized caching.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the data fetching logic to use TanStack Query with proper error handling and invalidation logic.'

steps.title: API Layer Hardening
steps.description: Ensure your backend is secure and performant by leveraging AI-driven code reviews.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current API routes for security vulnerabilities and optimize the database queries for N+1 performance issues.
steps.claude_code_command: claude review 'Audit the current API layer for security vulnerabilities and performance bottlenecks.'

steps.title: Continuous Integration Automation
steps.description: Automate the deployment pipeline to maintain high velocity and professional standards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow that runs unit tests, linting, and build validation on every push to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a .github/workflows/ci.yml file that automates testing and deployment for this project.'

pro_tips: Always define your 'System Prompt' in Claude Code to maintain your personal coding style and architectural preferences across every file.
Use Antigravity to generate 'Mock Data Factories' early; it forces you to clarify data structures before the UI gets messy.
Treat the AI's output as a junior dev's draft; never push without a manual 'sanity check' of the logic flow.
Leverage the 'Context Window' by keeping your documentation and schema files in a root /docs folder for the AI to reference.
When a feature gets complex, break it into smaller prompts to prevent the AI from losing track of state or context.
content.next_steps: Once your first AI-built module is functional, focus on building a custom 'Template Library' of your own components to make your next project 2x faster.
content.conversion_block: Stop settling for basic apps and start building the future of SaaS. Our community provides the prompts, the architecture, and the support you need to scale your freelance business. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
